content:
# Matthew Chapter 16 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_016_page_001.jpg, MAT_016_page_002.jpg

1. And having come, the Pharisaioi and Saddoukaioi, testing, asked him to show them a sign from the heaven.

2. And answering, he said to them, "When evening comes, you say, 'Fair weather, for the sky is red.'

3. And in the morning, 'Today [will be] stormy, for the sky is red and threatening.' The face of the sky you know [how] to discern, but the signs of the times you are not able?

4. An evil and adulterous generation seeks a sign, and a sign will not be given to it except the sign of Ionas." And having left them, he went away.

5. And the disciples, having come to the other side, forgot to take loaves.

6. And the Yeshua said to them, "Watch out and beware of the leaven of the Pharisaioi and Saddoukaioi."

7. And they were reasoning among themselves, saying, "Because we did not take loaves."

8. And having known, the Yeshua said, "Why do you reason among yourselves, [ones] of little faith, that you do not have loaves?

9. Do you not yet understand, nor remember the five loaves of the five thousand and how many baskets you took up?

10. Nor the seven loaves of the four thousand and how many baskets you took up?

11. How do you not understand that not concerning loaves I spoke to you? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisaioi and Saddoukaioi."

12. Then they understood that he did not say to beware of the leaven of the loaves, but of the teaching of the Pharisaioi and Saddoukaioi.

13. And having come, the Yeshua into the regions of Kaisareia of the Philippou, was asking the disciples of him, saying, "Who do the men say the Son of the Man to be?"

14. And they said, "Some [say] Ioannes the Baptizer, and others Elias, and others Ieremias or one of the prophets."

15. He says to them, "But you, who do you say me to be?"

16. And answering, Simon Petros said, "You are the Christos, the Son of the God, the living."

17. And answering, the Yeshua said to him, "Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Ionas, for flesh and blood did not reveal [this] to you, but the Father of me, the [one] in the heavens.

18. And I also say to you that you are Petros, and upon this rock I will build the assembly of me, and [the] gates of Hades will not overpower it.

19. I will give to you the keys of the kingdom of the heavens, and whatever you bind upon the earth will be having been bound in the heavens, and whatever you loose upon the earth will be having been loosed in the heavens."

20. Then he commanded the disciples that they should tell no one that he is the Christos.

21. From then the Yeshua began to show to the disciples of him that it is necessary for him to go to Hierosolyma and to suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and to be killed, and on the third day to be raised.

22. And having taken him aside, the Petros began to rebuke him, saying, "Merciful to you, Lord; this will not be to you."

23. And having turned, he said to the Petros, "Get behind me, Satanas! A scandal you are to me, for you do not think the things of the God but the things of men."

24. Then the Yeshua said to the disciples of him, "If anyone wishes to come after me, let him deny himself and take up the cross of him and follow me.

25. For whoever wishes to save the soul of him will lose it; but whoever loses the soul of him on account of me will find it.

26. For what will a man be profited if he gains the whole world but forfeits the soul of him? Or what will a man give [as] an exchange for the soul of him?

27. For the Son of the Man is about to come in the glory of the Father of him with the angels of him, and then he will repay each according to the practice of him.

28. Truly I say to you that there are some of those standing here who will not taste death until they see the Son of the Man coming in the kingdom of him."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Πέτρος / πέτρα (v.18):** "Petros / rock" — a wordplay. Πέτρος (Petros, masculine) is the name; πέτρα (petra, feminine) is the rock. Whether "this rock" refers to Peter himself, his confession, or Christ is debated. The Aramaic behind both would be כֵּיפָא (Kepha/Cephas), a single word.

2. **ἐκκλησία (v.18):** "Assembly" — rendered literally rather than "church." The Greek ἐκκλησία means a called-out assembly or congregation. This is the first of only two uses in the Gospels (the other in 18:17), both unique to Matthew.

3. **vv.18-19 — Binding and loosing:** Rabbinic technical terms for authoritative legal decisions — declaring what is forbidden (binding) and what is permitted (loosing). The perfect passive periphrastic ("will be having been bound/loosed") suggests the heavenly decision precedes or confirms the earthly one.

4. **Ἱλεώς σοι (v.22):** "Merciful to you" — an idiomatic expression meaning "May God be merciful to you!" or "God forbid!" Peter's rebuke reveals he cannot accept a suffering Messiah.

5. **ψυχή (vv.25-26):** "Soul/life" — the same dual-meaning word as in 10:39. The paradox of saving/losing one's ψυχή is central to Jesus' teaching on discipleship.

— EMET, AI Translator
